redcowBoundary: Affordance tags are compositional hints only. They are not observed probabilities, audience claims, platform weights, performance scores, or per-video REDCOW assessments.

L0.label: local or already owned
L0.rule: Uses owned media, code-native graphics, local tools, and no required metered generation.

L1.label: licensed stock or free-account source
L1.rule: Requires asset-level license capture or a free stock/API account but no required metered generation.

L2.label: metered generation or specialist compute
L2.rule: Likely uses metered image, video, voice, or specialist rendering; verify price and terms at execution time.

L3.label: bespoke production
L3.rule: Requires a custom shoot, presenter, actor, location, or substantial manual production.

sources.notes: Direct raw checkout is authoritative; npm installations are not used for registry truth.
Asset-level provenance is still required; do not imply endorsement or misuse identifiable people.

sources.licenseReference: MIT license displayed by the repository
sources.notes: Useful reference for TTS, captions, Pexels sourcing, music, and Remotion composition; visual quality is not assumed.
Format grammar only. Reject its downloaded-gameplay, API-key rotation, cookie transfer, unofficial uploader, and 2FA-avoidance patterns.
Useful longform-selection and vertical-crop pattern; repository claims MIT in README but no root LICENSE was visible during review.
Discovery reference for captions, assets, transitions, composition management, and parametric video; marketplace popularity is not a rights grant.
Chat-video interaction pattern only. Build a generic code-native interface; do not copy WhatsApp trade dress or repository code without a license.

archetypes.name: Owned Footage Proof
whenToUse: The thesis is stronger when Calvin, a participant, or a real environment is visible.
A source recording already contains a specific claim, action, demonstration, or correction.
whenNotToUse: No owned or explicitly licensed source recording exists.
The clip is mostly dead air and cannot support a complete proof-bearing beat without fabricating context.

beatGrammar.purpose: Open on the strongest owned visual or spoken proof.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use the source frame immediately; no generic title slate.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Retain intelligible source dialogue or start narration on the first frame.

beatGrammar.purpose: Name the situation and the exact claim being tested.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use a restrained label or owned cutaway that adds missing context.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Remove filler and preserve semantic continuity.

beatGrammar.purpose: Show what changed, failed, or was built.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Alternate source footage with owned evidence or a code-native diagram only when it explains the action.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Prioritize dialogue; duck music beneath speech.

beatGrammar.purpose: Land the result or correction.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Return to the clearest source frame and show the outcome without a decorative montage.
beatGrammar.audioRule: End on the completed sentence; no synthetic cliff-hanger.

inputs.sourceRule: Exact owned source file with item-level provenance and approved edit range.
Use source dialogue or a separately consented narration recording.
Only screenshots Calvin owns or may display, with private data redacted before render.

rights.transformRule: Edit for clarity and pacing while preserving the speaker's meaning, chronology, and consent scope.
hardStops: Edit changes the meaning of the source statement

audio.mixRule: Source dialogue leads; music is optional and must remain below speech without masking consonants.

implementation.nextGap: Add a public, rights-cleared authentic-footage showcase with exact source-range provenance.

layers.content: Owned vertical or reframed source footage
One specific context or result label

qualityRisks: Caption timing drifts from source dialogue

archetypes.name: Screen Proof Teardown
whenToUse: The claim can be proven with a product screen, code path, chart, error, diff, or before-and-after state.
whenNotToUse: The screen contains private data that cannot be safely redacted.
The thesis is emotional or experiential and the interface would only be filler.

beatGrammar.purpose: Open on the exact broken or surprising screen state.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Crop tightly to the evidence and highlight one region.
beatGrammar.audioRule: State what the viewer is looking at in plain language.

beatGrammar.purpose: Walk through the smallest causal path.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use cursor, zoom, diff, or callout; never animate unrelated UI.
beatGrammar.audioRule: One spoken explanation per visible action.

beatGrammar.purpose: Show the intervention or correction.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Keep the relevant before/after states visually comparable.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Use a subtle action SFX only if it marks the actual change.

beatGrammar.purpose: End on observable proof.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Show the passing state, changed output, or exact result label.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Name the verification, not a generic CTA.

inputs.sourceRule: Owned recording with secrets, account identifiers, and private data removed before ingestion.
Owned or licensed evidence frame with a documented redaction review.
Use only for arrows, zooms, diffs, and causal labels tied to the evidence.

rights.transformRule: Redact first, then crop and annotate only the proof-bearing regions; retain enough context to avoid a misleading result.
hardStops: Annotation implies a result not present in the recording

audio.mixRule: Narration owns the explanation; action SFX may mark real clicks or state changes but must not simulate proof.

implementation.nextGap: Create a dedicated proof-crop composition with deterministic redaction and callout anchors.

archetypes.name: Stock B-Roll Explainer
whenToUse: A public-safe topic needs broad visual context and no owned proof footage exists.
whenNotToUse: The claim depends on an exact product, person, event, or mechanism that generic stock cannot prove.
Search results would force unrelated keyword wallpaper.

beatGrammar.purpose: State one concrete question, contrast, or fact.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use the closest literal licensed clip, not a mood montage.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Narration begins immediately.

beatGrammar.purpose: Advance one idea per source clip.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Each clip must add setting, action, object, or consequence.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Change visuals on semantic boundaries rather than arbitrary intervals.

beatGrammar.purpose: Introduce a diagram, label, or number when stock alone is ambiguous.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use code-native graphics over or between clips.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Name the relationship shown by the graphic.

beatGrammar.purpose: End with a concise conclusion or next check.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Return to the most specific visual; avoid a generic sunset or handshake.
beatGrammar.audioRule: No unsupported urgency or sales close.

inputs.sourceRule: Capture asset URL, author, license, retrieval date, hash, and modifications for every clip.
Add only when it explains a relationship stock cannot show.
Use owned narration or a separately authorized generated voice.

rights.transformRule: Sequence licensed clips into an original explanation, keep asset-level provenance, and avoid endorsement or sensitive-person implications.
hardStops: Identifiable person shown in a harmful or misleading context

audio.mixRule: Narration leads; music supplies continuity but never compensates for weak visual relevance.

implementation.nextGap: Improve semantic source matching and show asset provenance in the public demo review.

layers.content: One rights-cleared literal stock clip per semantic beat

qualityRisks: Visual changes do not follow meaning

archetypes.name: UI-Native Thread Reveal
whenToUse: The story is inherently a sequence of messages, comments, commits, receipts, or decisions.
whenNotToUse: The UI is decorative and the same story would be clearer as direct narration.
The source contains private messages without permission or safe fictionalization.

beatGrammar.purpose: Open on the message that creates the question.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Show one large readable bubble or card.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Optional message sound must be subtle and consistent.

beatGrammar.purpose: Reveal only messages that change the interpretation.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Animate the thread vertically with stable sender positions.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Narration may bridge context but must not race ahead of the visible text.

beatGrammar.purpose: Show the decisive response, diff, or evidence.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Pause motion and highlight the exact line.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Use silence or one restrained emphasis sound at the reveal.

beatGrammar.purpose: Explain what the thread proves.
beatGrammar.visualRule: End on a generic summary card, not copied platform chrome.
beatGrammar.audioRule: State the interpretation and uncertainty plainly.

inputs.sourceRule: Use fictional, owned, consented, or safely redacted text; do not scrape private messages.
Render a generic interface with original tokens and no copied platform marks or trade dress.
A real screenshot requires ownership, redaction, and contextual review; prefer reconstruction.

rights.transformRule: Rebuild the interaction in a generic code-native UI, minimize quoted text, redact identities, and preserve the source meaning.
hardStops: Copied platform branding or trade dress

audio.mixRule: Narration is optional when the thread is self-contained; message SFX must not fire on every bubble.

implementation.nextGap: Add a generic thread component that explicitly avoids WhatsApp, Reddit, and Instagram trade dress.

layers.content: Generic original thread UI with two sender columns

qualityRisks: Private or fabricated material appears authentic

archetypes.name: Generated Image Microstory
whenToUse: A fictional, historical, abstract, or impossible-to-shoot sequence benefits from consistent illustrative frames.
whenNotToUse: Real proof or a real person is required.
Generated images could be mistaken for documentary evidence.
The concept lacks a shot-to-shot continuity plan.

beatGrammar.purpose: Establish the visual premise in one unmistakable image.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use a single subject, setting, and camera grammar.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Narration identifies fiction, reconstruction, or illustration when needed.

beatGrammar.purpose: Show one meaningful state change per frame.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Carry character, palette, props, and spatial continuity forward.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Scene changes follow sentence or clause boundaries.

beatGrammar.purpose: Reveal the consequence or contradiction.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use the largest compositional change only at the narrative turn.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Avoid trailer-style overstatement.

beatGrammar.purpose: Land the idea and disclose generated media when required.
beatGrammar.visualRule: End with a clear final state and optional provenance label.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Narration closes the story rather than looping artificially.

inputs.sourceRule: Every image needs provider/model/prompt/retrieval metadata, exact hash, and human review for identity and rights risks.
Original or licensed script with no imitation of a living creator's distinctive expression.
Use a licensed voice or consented synthetic voice with required disclosure and lineage.

rights.transformRule: Generate original frames from an owned storyboard, retain provider lineage, and label reconstructions where viewers could infer documentary truth.
hardStops: Generated frame presented as real evidence
Style imitation request targeting a living creator

audio.mixRule: Narration carries continuity; music and SFX support state changes without manufacturing realism.

implementation.nextGap: Add a first-class slideshow composition with continuity checks and visible provenance metadata in review artifacts.

layers.content: One reviewed generated image with gentle camera motion
Illustration or reconstruction label when needed

archetypes.name: Generated Video Concept
whenToUse: The motion itself communicates an impossible, speculative, or previsualized concept that owned footage cannot show.
whenNotToUse: A real product result, event, person, or experiment must be proven.
A still image or code-native diagram would communicate the mechanism more honestly and cheaply.

beatGrammar.purpose: Make the generated or conceptual nature clear when material could be read as real.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use the strongest reviewed shot with a concise label.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Narration frames the clip as concept, simulation, or illustration.

beatGrammar.purpose: Use motion to show the relationship that justified video generation.
beatGrammar.visualRule: One subject action and one camera intention per shot.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Describe the relationship, not visual spectacle alone.

beatGrammar.purpose: Connect the generated motion to the thesis or a real constraint.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use a code-native card, owned screen, or second generated shot with stable continuity.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Name what is hypothetical versus observed.

beatGrammar.purpose: End on the takeaway and provenance cue.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Freeze or settle on a legible final composition.
beatGrammar.audioRule: No claim that the generated scene actually occurred.

inputs.sourceRule: Provider/model/prompt/input asset/retrieval/exact-hash lineage and human review are mandatory.
Image-to-video inputs need their own provenance and stable rights-cleared delivery path.
Narration and music must be owned, licensed, or separately generated with provenance.

rights.transformRule: Treat outputs as generated assets, preserve the full lineage, disclose synthetic scenes when context could imply reality, and never use them as proof of a real outcome.
hardStops: Provider terms not checked for intended use

audio.mixRule: Narration distinguishes concept from fact; generated ambience must not imply documentary capture.

implementation.nextGap: Wire a provider-neutral generated-video lineage adapter into the local render and review harness.

layers.content: Reviewed generated motion with one clear subject action

archetypes.name: Motion Card Lesson
whenToUse: A concept, framework, comparison, or mechanism is clearer as designed text, shapes, cards, or diagrams.
whenNotToUse: Real-world proof is available and the graphics would hide it.
The lesson is only animated typography with no visual relationship.

beatGrammar.purpose: State the question or broken model.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use one dominant card and one relationship.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Narration names the misconception or task.

beatGrammar.purpose: Introduce components in causal order.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Animate persistent objects; do not reset the entire scene for each sentence.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Each spoken clause maps to one visual change.

beatGrammar.purpose: Show the comparison, flow, or state change.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Move, connect, group, or replace elements to express the mechanism.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Use SFX only on semantically meaningful transitions.

beatGrammar.purpose: End on a compact reusable takeaway.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Compose one screenshotable summary card with no internal production labels.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Read the takeaway once and stop.

inputs.sourceRule: Original SVG, HTML/CSS, React, or Remotion primitives with source-controlled design tokens.
Original concise lesson with evidence references outside the render when required.
Owned or separately authorized narration and sound design.

rights.transformRule: Build original graphic composition from primitives; trace any external icon, font, or illustration through the asset ledger.
hardStops: Animation does not encode the stated relationship

audio.mixRule: Narration and visual state changes stay synchronized; transition sounds are sparse and functional.

implementation.nextGap: Create reusable relationship primitives for cause, contrast, sequence, hierarchy, and feedback.

layers.content: Persistent cards, nodes, or comparison columns

archetypes.name: Spatial 3D Explainer
whenToUse: Depth, occlusion, assembly, camera movement, or a genuinely spatial relationship is essential to the explanation.
whenNotToUse: The idea is a flat list, timeline, text comparison, or decorative logo reveal.
A 2D diagram communicates the same relationship more legibly.

beatGrammar.purpose: Establish the object, space, and viewer orientation.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use one stable camera and visible reference frame.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Name the spatial relationship before moving the camera.

beatGrammar.purpose: Separate the component or path under discussion.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use opacity, cutaway, or exploded view rather than rapid orbiting.
beatGrammar.audioRule: One narration clause per isolated component.

beatGrammar.purpose: Demonstrate assembly, motion, flow, or depth-dependent change.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Camera movement follows the mechanism and stops before the next explanation.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Use restrained mechanical SFX only when they clarify an action.

beatGrammar.purpose: Return to the complete system and state the takeaway.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Reassemble or restore the reference view with one final label.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Conclude with the spatial insight, not the rendering process.

inputs.sourceRule: Original 3D scene or rights-cleared models with material and texture provenance.
Reference images or scans must be owned or explicitly licensed and safe to model.
Owned or licensed narration and sound design.

rights.transformRule: Use original or rights-cleared models, materials, textures, fonts, and audio; 3D must encode a spatial claim rather than decorate it.
hardStops: Camera motion causes loss of orientation

audio.mixRule: Narration establishes orientation; mechanical SFX mark real transformations without cinematic overload.

implementation.nextGap: Build one spatially justified Three.js or Remotion 3D composition and reject decorative 3D requests in its skill contract.

layers.content: Stable spatial stage with one object system

archetypes.name: Post Over Full-Screen Gameplay
whenToUse: A user-authored or permissioned post/story needs a visually active but semantically secondary background.
whenNotToUse: The gameplay is downloaded from a creator or game video without rights.
The story needs evidence, product proof, or source footage that gameplay would obscure.

beatGrammar.purpose: Establish continuous motion from frame one.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Full-screen licensed or procedural gameplay fills the phone frame.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Narration starts immediately; gameplay audio is muted or very low.

beatGrammar.purpose: Present the title or setup briefly.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use a generic original post card for the opening only.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Read or summarize the hook without copying unnecessary text.

beatGrammar.purpose: Carry the story with captions while gameplay remains secondary.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Remove the card and preserve a clear caption lane over uninterrupted gameplay.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Narration drives pacing; no random B-roll inserts.

beatGrammar.purpose: Land the final decision, reveal, or question.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use a concise final caption or card without interrupting gameplay continuity.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Resolve the spoken sentence; avoid fake loop truncation.

inputs.sourceRule: Original, permissioned, licensed, or safely transformed text; public posts are not automatically reusable scripts.
Gameplay must be owned, explicitly licensed, or supplied with a usable license and asset ledger.
Prefer original procedural gameplay when external gameplay rights are unclear.
Use an original generic post card rather than copied Reddit interface assets.

rights.transformRule: Use original or permissioned story text, code-native post UI, and owned/licensed/procedural gameplay; keep creator examples as pattern inspiration only.
hardStops: Scraped post used verbatim without review
Copied Reddit branding or user identity

audio.mixRule: Narration is primary; gameplay audio stays muted or low and music never competes with captions.

implementation.nextGap: Rename legacy brainrot template ids in a backward-compatible migration and keep the lane non-default outside story requests.

layers.content: Owned, licensed, or procedural continuous gameplay
Generic post card visible only for the setup

archetypes.name: Story Support Plus Gameplay Split
whenToUse: The story benefits from visible support footage while gameplay supplies continuous secondary motion.
The user explicitly requests a split-screen confession or storytime treatment.
whenNotToUse: Only one source layer is meaningful.
The vertical split makes either source unreadable or introduces gutters.
The format is chosen by habit rather than thesis fit.

beatGrammar.purpose: Establish the story-support layer and continuous gameplay without ambiguity.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Top and bottom panels fill the frame edge-to-edge with no gutter.
beatGrammar.audioRule: One narration track owns the story; source support audio is muted unless essential.

beatGrammar.purpose: Introduce the situation and characters.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Top support footage changes only when it adds story information; gameplay remains continuous.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Captions follow narration and stay out of panel boundaries.

beatGrammar.purpose: Advance the conflict or confession.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use support footage, simple labels, or a brief receipt; do not add a third filler source.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Music stays below narration and source dialogue.

beatGrammar.purpose: Land the reveal or decision.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Hold the clearest support frame while gameplay continues beneath.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Complete the resolution without a forced loop.

inputs.sourceRule: Top support footage must be owned, licensed, or generated with provenance and relate directly to the story.
Bottom gameplay must be owned or explicitly licensed; public creator uploads are not stock.
Use an original procedural loop when gameplay rights are unclear.
Original or permissioned story text with an explicit disclosure posture for fiction.

rights.transformRule: Every panel must have its own provenance; combine only rights-cleared assets and preserve the story's factual or fictional status.
hardStops: Downloaded Subway Surfers or creator footage

audio.mixRule: One narration track leads; support and gameplay source audio remain muted unless an approved story beat requires dialogue.

implementation.nextGap: Rebuild the public demo without gutters and prove OCR-safe captions before promotion.

layers.content: Story-specific owned or licensed support footage
Captions in the controlled center band

archetypes.name: Evidence Reaction
whenToUse: A source excerpt, claim, screenshot, chart, or clip needs contextual commentary, correction, or verification.
whenNotToUse: The source is used mainly for entertainment rather than analysis.
The excerpt cannot be licensed, quoted, or reviewed under the intended use.
Commentary merely repeats the source.

beatGrammar.purpose: Show the minimum excerpt needed to understand the claim.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Display a bounded excerpt with source label and no copied creator branding beyond what review requires.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Use only the minimal source audio necessary, or read a short attributed excerpt.

beatGrammar.purpose: State the precise agreement, disagreement, or question.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Switch to owned commentary, code-native annotation, or split view.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Commentary must add analysis immediately.

beatGrammar.purpose: Supply proof, counterexample, mechanism, or uncertainty.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use owned screen proof, document excerpt, or diagram with exact attribution.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Distinguish sourced fact from inference.

beatGrammar.purpose: Conclude with the corrected or qualified claim.
beatGrammar.visualRule: End on a concise original verdict card and source reference.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Avoid pile-on language and unsupported certainty.

inputs.sourceRule: Minimal attributed excerpt with rights or quotation review; do not download and repost whole creator clips.
Owned reaction, narration, or explanation footage with consent.
Public or licensed evidence excerpt with precise source and no unnecessary personal data.
Original annotations and verdict cards.

rights.transformRule: Use the minimum necessary source excerpt, add substantial original analysis, attribute clearly, and run jurisdiction-appropriate quotation/commentary review.
hardStops: Source identity obscured where attribution is required

audio.mixRule: Source audio is minimal and bounded; original commentary takes over immediately and remains dominant.

implementation.nextGap: Add an excerpt-duration and attribution review gate that is independent of platform download mechanics.

archetypes.name: Product Screen Demo
whenToUse: A real product workflow, output, or before-and-after can be demonstrated on screen.
whenNotToUse: The product is not ready to show, the capture is simulated, or the result cannot be verified.
The video would rely on claims rather than observable product behavior.

beatGrammar.purpose: Show the exact starting problem or friction.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use a real owned screen, input, or before state.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Name the task without exaggerated stakes.

beatGrammar.purpose: Demonstrate the smallest successful workflow.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Crop to the active region and show each real state change.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Narration follows, not precedes, the visible action.

beatGrammar.purpose: Show the exact product output or result.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Hold the output long enough to inspect and add one factual label.
beatGrammar.audioRule: State what was produced and any meaningful constraint.

beatGrammar.purpose: Clarify who should use it and when it is not a fit.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use a concise original end card or return to the product result.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Use a transparent CTA only when an offer or affiliation is actually present.

inputs.sourceRule: Owned product screen recording from a safe demo account with private data removed.
Capture the real action and result; simulated UI must be labeled as prototype or concept.
Use arrows, zooms, and labels only to clarify the real workflow.

rights.transformRule: Demonstrate real product behavior from a safe account, retain the actual outcome, and label prototypes or simulated states explicitly.

audio.mixRule: Narration tracks the real workflow; click SFX are sparse and may not imply a state change that did not occur.

implementation.nextGap: Add a deterministic before/action/output screen composition with redaction and proof-hold checks.

layers.content: Owned product capture with visible real state changes

archetypes.name: Deliberate Hybrid
whenToUse: One thesis genuinely requires two or more distinct proof roles, such as owned footage plus screen proof plus a diagram.
whenNotToUse: The mix exists only to increase visual change.
A single source lane can carry the whole explanation.
Any source lacks rights, provenance, or a specific semantic role.

beatGrammar.purpose: Open on the strongest real or explanatory anchor.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Choose exactly one primary source lane for the opening.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Narration names the thesis and the visible source.

beatGrammar.purpose: Move to a second source only when the proof role changes.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use a motivated transition and keep labels consistent across source types.
beatGrammar.audioRule: The sentence explains why the visual source changed.

beatGrammar.purpose: Combine the sources into one mechanism or comparison.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Use a code-native diagram, split view, or matched before/after; never stack every source at once.
beatGrammar.audioRule: Narration distinguishes observation, inference, and illustration.

beatGrammar.purpose: Close on the original anchor with the new meaning visible.
beatGrammar.visualRule: Return to the strongest proof or a compact takeaway card.
beatGrammar.audioRule: End with one conclusion and no unrelated montage.

inputs.sourceRule: Owned footage may serve as the primary proof or human anchor.
Owned redacted screen proof may serve as the action or result layer.
Stock is allowed only for context that cannot be shown with a more specific source.
Original graphics provide connective tissue and must encode a real relationship.
Generated imagery is illustration only and needs full provenance.
Generated video is used only when motion is essential and cannot be mistaken for proof.

rights.transformRule: Assign every asset one semantic role, retain asset-level provenance, disclose generated material when needed, and remove any source that does not advance the thesis.
hardStops: Source switch has no semantic reason
More than one dominant layer competes for attention

audio.mixRule: One narration spine unifies the source changes; source audio appears only when it is the evidence.

implementation.nextGap: Add a source-role planner that fails when a requested asset has no declared semantic job.

layers.content: One primary source at a time: owned footage, screen proof, or licensed context
